label: AppleScript • liveProof chained read-then-record
query: Read the current system output volume, then record it in a note named 'Osaurus Volume Log' (create the note if it doesn't exist), then read that note's body back and report it.
notes: Ground-truth lane for the chained read-one-place-write-another shape: the REAL model + REAL executor read actual system state (output volume) and persist it into actual Notes, then prove it landed by reading the note back. HAS REAL SIDE EFFECTS — it writes a dedicated scratch note — and needs Notes Automation permission, so it is meant to be run locally, permission-gated. A pass requires the run to SUCCEED with a read-back containing the recorded value: a run whose only script errors and whose final reply claims completion anyway must fail here (that hallucinated-done shape is exactly what this row measures — the 16B currently fails it; see the capability scoreboard). Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

rubric: The AppleScript reads the real current system output volume (not a hardcoded guess) and writes that value into the note named 'Osaurus Volume Log', creating the note if missing.
The reported read-back contains the recorded volume value — the workflow proves the value was persisted, not merely read.
